title: Filipino Smoky Potato Dumplings
description: A twist on traditional Filipino adobo with smoky potatoes and served in a dumpling. This dish combines flavors from Filipino cuisine with the comfort of a potato dumpling.
instructions: Boil a large pot of water and add a tablespoon of salt.
Cook the potatoes in the boiling water for 10-12 minutes, or until they're tender.
Drain the potatoes and mash them in a large bowl.
Add the savory dumpling wrappers, adobo spices, soy sauce, rice vinegar, smoked paprika, and salt to the bowl.
Mix the ingredients together until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it's smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces.
Shape each piece into a ball and then flatten it slightly into a disk.
Place a tablespoon of water on one half of the disk and fold the other half over to form a half-moon shape.
Press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Repeat with the remaining dough and water.
Serve the dumplings hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
